record.field: Strapi is the leading open-source headless CMS that turns your content model into ready-to-use REST and GraphQL APIs.

Strapi is the leading open-source headless CMS that turns your content model into ready-to-use REST and GraphQL APIs.

Built entirely in JavaScript/TypeScript, Strapi lets developers design content structures visually with the Content-Type Builder and instantly exposes them through auto-generated APIs, while editors manage content from a customizable admin panel. It is API-first and front-end agnostic, pairing naturally with frameworks like Next.js, Nuxt, and Astro, and it can run fully self-hosted with the database and storage of your choice.

Key features
  • Visual Content-Type Builder with no schema coding required
  • Auto-generated REST and GraphQL APIs for every content type
  • Role-based access control, fine-grained permissions, and a media library
  • Draft & publish workflows and built-in internationalization (i18n)
  • Extensible plugin architecture and a plugin marketplace
  • Database flexibility: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, or SQLite
Best for

Development teams and agencies building websites, mobile apps, or multi-channel digital products that need a self-hosted, API-driven content backend with full ownership of their data and infrastructure.

services: strapi: image: elestio/strapi-production:v5.33.0 environment: NODE_ENV: production STRAPI_TELEMETRY_DISABLED: true STRAPI_PLUGIN_I18N_INIT_LOCALE_CODE: en FAST_REFRESH: true JWT_SECRET: {{ jwt_secret }} ADMIN_JWT_SECRET: {{ admin_jwt_secret }} DATABASE_CLIENT: postgres DATABASE_HOST: strapi_postgres DATABASE_PORT: 5432 DATABASE_NAME: strapi DATABASE_USERNAME: strapi DATABASE_PASSWORD: {{ postgres_password }} volumes: - ./strapi_data:/srv/app expose: - 1337 depends_on: - strapi_postgres healthcheck: test: - CMD - wget - -q - --spider - http://127.0.0.1:1337 start_period: 3s interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 5 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' restart: unless-stopped ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:1337' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network strapi_postgres: image: postgres:18 environment: POSTGRES_DB: strapi POSTGRES_USER: strapi POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ postgres_password }} volumes: - ./strapi_postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U $${POSTGRES_USER} -d $${POSTGRES_DB} start_period: 3s interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 5 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_strapi_postgres' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T strapi_postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d strapi_postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T strapi_postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T strapi_postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T strapi_postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
